_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to take turns speaking and listening in group conversations, using the right voice level for the situation.

_source.w: We are learning to take turns speaking and listening in group conversations, using the right voice level for the situation.
y: Good conversations need both speakers and listeners — learning to do both makes us effective communicators.
Using the right voice level shows respect for others and helps our message be heard.
These skills help us participate fully in class discussions and group work.
eg: We practise putting our hand up to speak and waiting for our turn in class discussions.
We share ideas in pairs, practising listening carefully while our partner speaks.
We use a quiet indoor voice during class and a louder voice outside.
We play a game where we pass a talking stick — only the person holding it can speak.
We practise looking at the person who is talking to show we are listening.
We contribute an idea during a class meeting and listen to what others say afterwards.
